movements.name: Hip Circles
movements.notes: Warming hip joint through full range of motion

movements.name: Shoulder CARs
movements.notes: Controlled articular rotations for shoulder mobility

strength.label: Power Clean Complex — Ramp to Heavy

movements.name: Power Clean
movements.notes: Progressively increase load: Set 1 @ 65% x3, Set 2 @ 72% x2, Set 3 @ 78% x2, Set 4 @ 83% x1, Set 5 @ 85% x1. Focus on explosive hip extension and fast elbows.
movements.scaling: Hang power clean or dumbbell power clean

movements.name: Power Clean
movements.notes: Barbell cycling under fatigue. Maintain technique.

movements.name: Pull-Up
movements.notes: Kipping allowed if athlete has strict pull-up base (3+ reps)

movements.name: Box Jump
movements.notes: Step down from box, full hip extension at top

metcon.target_score: Complete all reps in under 10 minutes

movements.name: Foam Roll — Quads
movements.notes: Soft tissue release after high-volume squatting pattern

movements.name: Pigeon Pose
movements.notes: Hip external rotation and glute stretch

session_why.text: This session targets power development through Olympic lifting followed by a mixed-modal metcon. The power clean builds rate of force development (Zatsiorsky & Kraemer), while the metcon applies that power output under fatigue. The 21-15-9 format provides descending volume that allows maintenance of intensity as glycogen depletes. Time domain of 8-12 minutes targets glycolytic system without excessive oxidative demand that would interfere with strength adaptation.
session_why.source: Zatsiorsky & Kraemer - Science and Practice of Strength Training, Wilson et al 2012 - Concurrent Training Meta-Analysis

movement_why.text: Power clean is the primary strength movement, training the full kinetic chain from hinge through pull to receiving position. Pull-ups and box jumps in the metcon maintain the power emphasis — pull-ups develop vertical pulling strength (CrossFit's 10 physical skills), and box jumps train lower body power output. This combination addresses strength, power, and coordination across multiple planes.
movement_why.source: Glassman 2002 - CrossFit Journal (10 Physical Skills), Bompa & Haff - Periodization 5th ed

loading_why.text: Ramp/build-up loading scheme is used for Week 1 Olympic lifting to prepare the nervous system progressively for heavier loads while refining technique. Starting at 65% allows technical focus; ending at 85% provides neural stimulus without excessive CNS fatigue. Metcon load is reduced to 60% 1RM to allow barbell cycling while maintaining movement quality under fatigue. Rest periods of 2-3 minutes allow phosphocreatine recovery between power clean sets.
loading_why.source: Zatsiorsky & Kraemer - Science and Practice of Strength Training Ch.3, Haff & Triplett NSCA Essentials Ch.3

movements.name: Assault Bike
movements.notes: Elevate heart rate, prepare for cardio work

movements.name: Front Squat
movements.notes: Perform 4 reps at the top of each 2-minute window. EMOM format auto-regulates rest and maintains session flow. Focus on upright torso and full depth.
movements.scaling: Goblet squat or reduce load to 65-70%

movements.name: Assault Bike
movements.notes: Moderate pace — this is an aerobic AMRAP, not a sprint
movements.scaling: Reduce to 9 calories or substitute row

movements.name: Kettlebell Swing (Russian)
movements.notes: Hip hinge pattern, swing to eye level
movements.scaling: Reduce reps or use lighter kettlebell

movements.name: Burpee
movements.notes: Full chest-to-deck, jump and clap overhead

metcon.target_score: Target 6-8 rounds. Steady pace, not maximal effort.

movements.name: Thoracic Rotation
movements.notes: Restore thoracic mobility after front rack position

session_why.text: This session prioritizes lower body structural strength through front squat volume followed by a longer aerobic AMRAP. The 15-minute time domain places primary demand on the oxidative system (Gastin 2001), developing aerobic capacity while minimizing interference with the strength work. The AMRAP format teaches pacing and work capacity management. This session fits the weekly requirement for at least one aerobic-emphasis piece.
session_why.source: Gastin 2001 - Energy System Interaction, Wilson et al 2012 - Concurrent Training Meta-Analysis

movement_why.text: Front squat develops squat pattern strength with anterior loading, improving thoracic extension and core stability. The metcon combines monostructural cardio (assault bike), hinge pattern (KB swing), and full-body movement (burpee) to distribute load across multiple muscle groups, preventing localized fatigue. This variety sustains movement quality over 15 minutes and addresses CrossFit's broad modal training principle.
movement_why.source: Glassman 2002 - CrossFit Journal, Haff & Triplett NSCA Essentials 4th ed

loading_why.text: E2MOM strength format at 75% 1RM (Week 2 intensity) allows consistent volume accumulation without excessive rest periods. The 2-minute interval provides adequate phosphocreatine recovery for 4-rep sets at this intensity while maintaining session flow. EMOM format rewards efficient execution and prevents sandbagging. Metcon movements are intentionally moderate intensity to preserve aerobic pacing rather than glycolytic sprint efforts.
loading_why.source: Bompa & Haff - Periodization 5th ed, Haff & Triplett NSCA Essentials Ch.3

movements.name: Deadlift
movements.notes: Wave loading exploits post-activation potentiation. After heavy set at 85%, the 75% set will feel lighter due to neural priming. Maintain neutral spine throughout.
movements.scaling: Reduce intensity by 5-10% or use trap bar deadlift

movements.name: Deadlift
movements.notes: Touch-and-go reps allowed. Maintain lumbar position.
movements.scaling: Reduce load to 50% or reduce reps to 7

movements.name: Chest-to-Bar Pull-Up
movements.notes: Full chest contact with bar. Kipping allowed if strict base exists.

metcon.target_score: Complete 5 rounds in 7-9 minutes. High intensity couplet.

session_why.text: This session develops maximal pulling strength through wave loading deadlifts followed by a high-intensity sprint couplet. The 7-10 minute metcon time domain targets the glycolytic system (Gastin 2001), developing anaerobic capacity. The hinge + vertical pull pairing addresses posterior chain and back strength comprehensively. Short metcon duration minimizes interference with the heavy strength work.
session_why.source: Gastin 2001 - Energy System Interaction, Wilson et al 2012 - Concurrent Training Meta-Analysis

movement_why.text: Deadlift is the primary hinge movement, recruiting the entire posterior chain from calves through traps. Chest-to-bar pull-ups in the metcon develop vertical pulling strength and lat engagement under fatigue. This pairing ensures balanced pull volume (horizontal hinge + vertical pull) while maintaining movement specificity. The couplet format creates high work density without excessive movement complexity.
movement_why.source: Haff & Triplett NSCA Essentials 4th ed, Glassman 2002 - CrossFit Journal

loading_why.text: Wave loading is used for Week 2 intensity progression. Oscillating between 75-85-75-82-87% exploits post-activation potentiation — heavy sets at 85% and 87% prime the nervous system, making subsequent moderate loads feel lighter (Robbins 2005). This allows higher total volume at elevated intensities compared to straight sets. Metcon deadlift load is reduced to 60% to allow barbell cycling while preserving movement quality.
loading_why.source: Robbins 2005 - Post-Activation Potentiation, Bompa & Haff - Periodization 5th ed

movements.name: Inchworm
movements.notes: Dynamic warmup for shoulders and core

movements.name: Hollow Body Hold
movements.notes: Core tension activation for gymnastics work

strength.label: Strict Press — Top Set + Back-Off Volume

movements.name: Strict Press
movements.rest: 3 minutes after top set, 90 seconds between volume sets
movements.notes: Set 1: 2 reps at 87% (top set for neural stimulus). Sets 2-5: 5 reps at 70% (back-off volume for structural adaptation). This combines peak intensity with hypertrophy volume.
movements.scaling: Reduce top set to 80% and volume to 65%, or use dumbbell press

movements.name: Handstand Push-Up
movements.notes: Strict or kipping. Full depth to floor.
movements.scaling: Pike push-up or box pike push-up

movements.name: Box Jump
movements.notes: Full hip extension at top, step down

metcon.target_score: Complete all reps within each minute. Rest remaining time.

movements.name: Shoulder CARs
movements.notes: Restore shoulder range after pressing volume

session_why.text: This session develops vertical pressing strength through strict press while training gymnastics pressing and lower body power in the metcon. The EMOM format alternates upper body push (HSPU) with lower body power (box jumps), allowing localized recovery while maintaining cardiovascular demand. The 14-minute duration targets the glycolytic-oxidative transition zone, building work capacity without excessive systemic fatigue.
session_why.source: Glassman 2002 - CrossFit Journal, Gastin 2001 - Energy System Interaction

movement_why.text: Strict press is the foundational vertical push movement, developing shoulder strength and core stability. HSPU in the metcon applies vertical push under bodyweight in an inverted position, training balance and coordination (CrossFit physical skills). Box jumps develop lower body power and explosive hip extension. This pairing addresses upper body strength, gymnastics capacity, and lower body power in one session.
movement_why.source: Glassman 2002 - CrossFit Journal (10 Physical Skills), Sommer - Building the Gymnastic Body

loading_why.text: Top set + back-off volume scheme is appropriate for Week 3 peak intensity. The 87% top set provides maximal neural stimulus when the athlete is fresh, while the 70% back-off volume accumulates structural adaptation without excessive CNS fatigue. This scheme efficiently combines strength and hypertrophy work within time constraints. EMOM format in metcon enforces pacing discipline and prevents excessive rest.
loading_why.source: Bompa & Haff - Periodization 5th ed Ch.9, Zatsiorsky & Kraemer - Science and Practice of Strength Training

movements.name: Row
movements.notes: Elevate heart rate for metcon prep

movements.name: Hang Power Snatch
movements.load: E2MOM: Build from 60% to 80% 1RM over 8 sets
movements.notes: Start at 60% 1RM and add weight each set, finishing at ~80%. Focus on aggressive hip extension and fast turnover under the bar. This is technical power work, not max effort.
movements.scaling: Dumbbell snatch or reduce load progression

movements.name: Row
movements.scaling: Reduce to 12 calories or substitute assault bike

movements.name: Kettlebell Swing (American)
movements.notes: Full overhead lockout at top of swing

movements.name: Burpee
movements.notes: Chest to deck, jump and clap overhead

metcon.target_score: Complete 4 rounds in 7-9 minutes. Sprint pace.

session_why.text: This session develops Olympic lifting power through hang power snatch followed by a high-intensity mixed-modal sprint. The hang position simplifies the snatch to focus on explosive hip extension and speed under the bar. The 7-10 minute metcon targets the glycolytic system (Gastin 2001), building anaerobic capacity. The combination of monostructural cardio, hinge pattern, and full-body movement distributes load to prevent localized failure.
session_why.source: Gastin 2001 - Energy System Interaction, Haff & Triplett NSCA Essentials 4th ed

movement_why.text: Hang power snatch trains explosive triple extension and receiving speed without the technical complexity of the full snatch. Row develops aerobic power and posterior chain endurance. American KB swings maintain the hinge pattern under fatigue while adding overhead positional strength. Burpees provide full-body conditioning and ground-to-standing transitions. This combination addresses power, stamina, and coordination.
movement_why.source: Glassman 2002 - CrossFit Journal, Bompa & Haff - Periodization 5th ed

loading_why.text: E2MOM build format allows progressive loading from 60-80% while maintaining technique quality. Building load across sets provides progressive neural activation without the fatigue of heavy singles. The 2-minute interval ensures adequate recovery for power output. This is Week 1-2 appropriate loading — developing the movement pattern before pursuing maximal intensity. Metcon uses moderate kettlebell load to allow unbroken sets under high heart rate.
loading_why.source: Zatsiorsky & Kraemer - Science and Practice of Strength Training, Haff & Triplett NSCA Essentials Ch.3

movements.name: Romanian Deadlift (empty bar)
movements.notes: Hinge activation for paused RDL work

movements.name: Back Squat
movements.rest: Superset with RDL, then 2 minutes rest
movements.notes: Perform back squat, immediately transition to RDL, then rest 2 minutes before next round. Full depth squat, controlled descent.

movements.name: Romanian Deadlift (paused at knee)
movements.notes: 1-second pause at knee level on each rep. Eccentric control emphasis.

movements.name: Pull-Up
movements.notes: Kipping allowed if strict base exists (3+ strict reps)

metcon.target_score: Target 7-9 rounds. Moderate pace, sustainable effort.

movements.name: Foam Roll — Quads
movements.notes: Release quad tension from squat volume

session_why.text: This session develops both squat and hinge patterns in a time-efficient superset, addressing weekly movement balance requirements. The bodyweight AMRAP provides aerobic conditioning without additional loading after heavy strength work, minimizing interference effects (Wilson et al 2012). The 12-minute duration develops oxidative capacity while the calisthenic movements maintain movement quality throughout.
session_why.source: Wilson et al 2012 - Concurrent Training Meta-Analysis, Glassman 2002 - CrossFit Journal

movement_why.text: Back squat and Romanian deadlift address the two fundamental lower body patterns (squat and hinge) in one session. Paused RDL develops eccentric control and positional awareness. The metcon triplet of pull-up, push-up, and air squat ensures balanced push-pull volume and reinforces fundamental movement patterns under cardiovascular stress. This combination develops strength, muscular endurance, and coordination.
movement_why.source: Haff & Triplett NSCA Essentials 4th ed, Glassman 2002 - CrossFit Journal (10 Physical Skills)

loading_why.text: Straight sets at 72% (Week 2 loading) for back squat provide hypertrophy stimulus with manageable fatigue. Romanian deadlift at 60% with pause increases time under tension for eccentric strength development without excessive CNS demand. Supersetting these movements is safe because they target different movement patterns (squat vs hinge), allowing one pattern to recover while the other works. This is time-efficient volume accumulation.
loading_why.source: Bompa & Haff - Periodization 5th ed, Schoenfeld - Science and Development of Muscle Hypertrophy

movements.name: Ski Erg
movements.notes: Elevate heart rate, prime pulling pattern

movements.name: Push Press
movements.notes: Consistent load across all 5 sets. Focus on aggressive leg drive and lockout speed. Elbows high in the dip, punch the bar overhead.
movements.scaling: Dumbbell push press or reduce load to 70%

movements.name: Ski Erg
movements.scaling: Reduce to 10 calories or substitute row

movements.name: Kettlebell Clean
movements.notes: Alternating arms, clean to rack position
movements.scaling: Use lighter kettlebell or reduce reps

movements.name: Toes-to-Bar
movements.notes: Full contact of toes to bar. Kipping allowed.

metcon.target_score: Complete all reps in each minute, rest remaining time. Maintain consistent quality across all 5 rounds.

session_why.text: This session develops explosive overhead pressing through push press, then applies that pressing capacity in a varied EMOM alongside monostructural cardio, hinge pattern, and core work. The 15-minute EMOM with 3 distinct movements per round creates a mixed-modal stimulus that develops stamina and coordination (Glassman 2002). The rotating movement pattern prevents localized failure while maintaining cardiovascular demand.
session_why.source: Glassman 2002 - CrossFit Journal, Gastin 2001 - Energy System Interaction

movement_why.text: Push press combines leg drive with overhead pressing, developing power and full-body coordination. Ski erg provides upper body dominant monostructural cardio. KB cleans train hinge pattern and unilateral strength. Toes-to-bar develop core-to-extremity strength and lat engagement. This combination addresses vertical push, pull, hinge, and core across multiple energy systems, fitting the full-body category requirement.
movement_why.source: Glassman 2002 - CrossFit Journal (10 Physical Skills), Haff & Triplett NSCA Essentials 4th ed

loading_why.text: Straight sets at 75% (Week 2 loading) provide moderate intensity that builds pressing strength and volume without excessive fatigue. This allows quality execution across 5 sets. EMOM format in the metcon enforces work:rest discipline — athletes must complete work in 45-50 seconds to earn recovery time. The 3-movement rotation within each 3-minute block distributes load and allows partial recovery between similar demands.
loading_why.source: Bompa & Haff - Periodization 5th ed, Haff & Triplett NSCA Essentials Ch.17
